resolvedQuestions.question: Is the structural layout sound?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Yes, if runtime remains infrastructure under system-deep-loop/runtime and is not added as a workflow mode.

resolvedQuestions.question: Are bidirectional path-coupling rules correct?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Yes. Forward and reverse directions require different relative-depth repairs.

resolvedQuestions.question: Is the system-spec-kit tooling-borrow accounted for?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Yes, and it is load-bearing test/typecheck wiring scoped into 002.

resolvedQuestions.question: Are external references and advisor surfaces covered?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Yes, if 003 treats advisor/codegen/corpus/fixtures as first-class gates, not prose cleanup.

resolvedQuestions.question: Should fallback-router.ts be wired for GLM-to-MiMo fallback?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Not as an implicit merge requirement. It is valuable optional hardening with separate operator approval.

keyFindings.title: runtime/ must be infrastructure, not an eighth mode
keyFindings.summary: The proposed tree nests the former runtime under system-deep-loop/runtime and explicitly says adding runtime as a mode-registry entry would be a category error.

keyFindings.title: Path repair is intentionally asymmetric
keyFindings.summary: Forward runtime-to-workflow references keep hop count and delete the old workflow segment; reverse workflow-to-runtime references move one hop nearer and rename the segment.

keyFindings.title: The tooling-borrow is load-bearing
keyFindings.summary: Runtime package scripts and tsconfig borrow system-spec-kit tooling, while system-spec-kit vitest includes runtime tests. Those paths must move in 002.

keyFindings.title: External migration is executable, not just prose
keyFindings.summary: Doctor routes, deep command YAMLs, and both OpenCode/Claude agent mirrors carry old executable path references.

keyFindings.title: Advisor migration is the highest-risk reference area
keyFindings.summary: The advisor has generated projection/hash surfaces, hand constants, lexical scoring boosts, graph edges, corpus, and divergence fixtures.

keyFindings.title: fallback-router.ts needs an explicit caller if 004 is accepted
keyFindings.summary: The router enforces cross-pool and approved-model guards, while fanout-pool currently retries and marks retry exhaustion without resolving a fallback target.

keyFindings.title: GLM and MiMo are separate active pools but no fallback is configured
keyFindings.summary: MiMo and GLM model profiles both have active opencode executors but fallback_target is null for both; automatic routing would be new policy.

keyFindings.title: 003 has the right dependency ordering and exit gates
keyFindings.summary: The external migration plan requires baseline inventory, hardcoded constants first, generated projection/codegen, command-contract regeneration, corpus re-baseline, and full exit gates.

keyFindings.title: Proceed with 002 and 003, keep 004 optional
keyFindings.summary: The merge/reference-migration design is sound. Fallback-router wiring is real hardening but should remain separate unless the operator opts in.

ruledOutDirections.reason: Forward and reverse path repairs differ.
runtime is infrastructure, not a mode.
Optional feature scope needs operator decision.
